expectations.text: No raw <div> or <span> used as layout containers. Flex, Box, or Card from hearth-react used instead.
expectations.evidence: Component uses Card, CardContent, CardInteraction, Flex, IconContainer — all from hearth-react. No raw <div> or <span> present.

expectations.text: Card (or CardContent / CardActions sub-components) is imported from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-react and used as the outer container.
expectations.evidence: Imports Card, CardContent, CardInteraction from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-react. Card is the root element.

expectations.text:
All text content uses Hearth typography components (Heading, BodyText, DetailText). No raw

,

-

, or for text.
expectations.evidence: Uses Heading as='h2', BodyText, DetailText size='sm' for labels. BodyText weight='semibold' for cost display (rather than DetailText, but still a Hearth component).

expectations.text: The 'Manage broadband' link uses Link or CardInteraction/CardActionLink from hearth-react. No raw <a> tag.
expectations.evidence: <CardInteraction asChild><Link href={manageHref}>Manage broadband...</Link></CardInteraction> — uses both CardInteraction and Link from hearth-react.

expectations.text: The hub offline warning is conditionally rendered based on a prop, and uses a Hearth feedback component (Alert or similar) rather than a raw element.
expectations.evidence: {isSmartHubOffline && <Alert colorScheme='warning' title='Smart hub offline' text='...' />} — conditional on isSmartHubOffline prop, using Alert.

expectations.text: A TypeScript interface or type is defined for the component's props, covering at minimum packageName, monthlyCost, contractEndDate, and hubOffline.
expectations.evidence: BroadbandServiceCardProps defines: packageName (string), monthlyCost (number), contractEndDate (Date), manageHref (string), isSmartHubOffline (boolean, optional). All four required fields covered.

expectations.text: A BroadbandServiceCard.test.tsx file is present and contains at least one test.
expectations.evidence: BroadbandServiceCard.test.tsx present with 10 tests covering rendering, offline warning, and edge cases.

claims.claim: monthlyCost is typed as number with formatting handled by a utility function
claims.evidence: monthlyCost: number prop, formatCurrency helper using Intl.NumberFormat. Contrast with with_skill which accepts pre-formatted strings.

claims.claim: BodyText is used for the monthly cost value instead of DetailText
claims.evidence: <BodyText size='md' weight='semibold'>{formatCurrency(monthlyCost)} / month</BodyText> — BodyText rather than DetailText for the price, which is less idiomatic for a prominent price display.

suggestions.reason: The typography assertion doesn't distinguish between using BodyText vs DetailText for a price figure. Both pass, but DetailText is the more appropriate choice for a highlighted numeric value. A more discriminating assertion would check that the primary cost value uses DetailText.

eval_feedback.overall: All structural assertions pass. The without-skill output is competent but makes less idiomatic choices (BodyText for price instead of DetailText, raw number props instead of pre-formatted strings).
